THE_ARCHITECT.talk.SIEGFRIEDSABER-SIEGFR: A dragon's blood made you a wall, and a single leaf made you mortal again. The Spire admires such economy of weakness.
Pass, then, apologetic knight. Mind my floors — they have no back for you to guard, and no patience for your sorries.
Still murmuring 'forgive me' to the very stones you climb? Save it. The Spire forgives nothing, and remembers everything.
Draw Balmung if you must. I have weathered worse oaths than a hero's, and outlasted every dragon that swore them.
